title: Gauge and Instrument Display Research Catalog
scope_note: Mechanism-first research catalogue; generic framebuffer/HDMI displays excluded.

quirk_schema.qualifiers: Optional concise normalised qualifier; used where wording such as tapping needs an explicit mode.
quirk_schema.source_phrases: Exact source statements retained so no original behavioural detail is discarded.

normalisation.count_basis: A gauge is counted once for a canonical quirk even when several source phrases support it.
normalisation.compound_statement_policy: A source phrase may support multiple canonical quirks when it contains multiple behaviours.

date_confidence_legend.H: Dated primary, official or museum support
date_confidence_legend.M: Approximate adoption/common-use range from reliable overviews

gauge_group_legend.name: Radial pointer
gauge_group_legend.description: One or more hands sweep an arc or circle against a fixed scale.

gauge_group_legend.name: Linear pointer
gauge_group_legend.description: A pointer, blade, hairline or marker moves along a straight or edgewise scale.

gauge_group_legend.name: Liquid column
gauge_group_legend.description: A liquid height, meniscus or liquid/gas boundary moves in a tube, well or gauge glass.

gauge_group_legend.name: Floating indicator
gauge_group_legend.description: A buoyant float, bob or bulb directly indicates the value by its position.

gauge_group_legend.name: Free mass, leaf, ball or bubble
gauge_group_legend.description: A freely deflecting mass, leaf, ball, pendulum or bubble is itself the indicator.

gauge_group_legend.name: Rotating scale or scene
gauge_group_legend.description: A compass card, scale, drum, horizon or scene rotates behind a fixed reference.

gauge_group_legend.name: Moving tape, ribbon or map
gauge_group_legend.description: A strip, band, tape or printed map travels through a window or past a fixed index.

gauge_group_legend.name: Rolling drum or counter
gauge_group_legend.description: Numeral drums, cyclometer wheels or register wheels rotate to form a count or value.

gauge_group_legend.name: Chart or trace recorder
gauge_group_legend.description: A pen, stylus, light beam or scratch records a changing value on a moving medium.

gauge_group_legend.name: Resonant or oscillating element
gauge_group_legend.description: A vibrating reed, string or other oscillating element is observed directly.

gauge_group_legend.name: Projected spot or shadow
gauge_group_legend.description: A projected light spot, vane shadow or optical image moves across a scale.

gauge_group_legend.name: Optical null or match
gauge_group_legend.description: The reading is found by visually matching brightness, colour or disappearance at a null.

gauge_group_legend.name: Indicator lamp or illuminated legend
gauge_group_legend.description: A lamp, lens or illuminated legend indicates a state or coarse condition.

gauge_group_legend.name: Mechanical flag, shutter or semaphore
gauge_group_legend.description: A flag, shutter, vane, striped drum or semaphore changes between visible states.

gauge_group_legend.name: Flip-element array
gauge_group_legend.description: Bistable discs, dots, tiles or flags physically flip to form symbols or levels.

gauge_group_legend.name: Split-flap display
gauge_group_legend.description: Physical character flaps cycle through intermediate positions to reach the target.

gauge_group_legend.name: Segmented display
gauge_group_legend.description: Fixed luminous, gas-discharge, liquid-crystal or material segments form numbers or glyphs.

gauge_group_legend.name: Bar, column, wedge or moving-dot display
gauge_group_legend.description: Magnitude is shown as a continuous or discrete bar, wedge, column or advancing dot.

gauge_group_legend.name: Dot-matrix or cell array
gauge_group_legend.description: An addressable matrix of dots or cells forms characters, symbols or fields.

gauge_group_legend.name: Vector or storage trace
gauge_group_legend.description: A beam or equivalent trace draws waveforms, radar returns or stored vector imagery.

gauge_group_legend.name: Projected symbology
gauge_group_legend.description: Collimated or head-up symbols are projected into the operator’s field of view.

gauge_group_legend.name: Colour or material-state indicator
gauge_group_legend.description: Colour, opacity, scattering, phase or chemical state changes to indicate value or history.

gauge_group_legend.name: Rotating scan or strobe
gauge_group_legend.description: Timing is converted to apparent position by a rotating scan, flash, glow point or strobe.

gauge_group_legend.name: Pattern-forming or biological indicator
gauge_group_legend.description: Crystals, living organisms or other emergent patterns provide the indication.

entries.name: Liquid-column manometer: U-tube, well-type and inclined
entries.representative_names_models: Open/closed U-tubes; cistern/well manometers; inclined draft gauges
